name: Ground Turkey Chinese Spring Rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. In a medium nonstick skillet, cook and stir ground turkey, garlic, and ginger over medium-high heat for 4 to 5 minutes or until the turkey is no longer pink. Drain thoroughly.
3. In a medium bowl, combine the cooked turkey mixture with bok choy, onions, soy sauce, sherry, and sesame oil.
4. On a clean, dry counter, layer the phyllo sheets into a stack and cut into 2 rectangles measuring 18 x 7 inches. Work with one rectangle at a time. Keep the remaining phyllo sheets covered with a damp cloth following package directions.
5. Coat one rectangle of phyllo with nonstick cooking spray. Arrange the phyllo sheet on the counter so that the 7-inch side is parallel to the counter edge. Place about 1/4 cup of turkey mixture in a strip, approximately 1 inch away from the bottom and side edges of the phyllo.
6. Fold the bottom edge of the phyllo over the filling, then fold the longer edges of the phyllo toward the center and roll up tightly, forming a jelly-roll style spring roll.
7. Repeat the process with the remaining rectangles of phyllo and filling to make the remaining spring rolls.
8. Place the rolls on two cookie sheets coated with nonstick cooking spray, seam side down. Coat the tops of the rolls with nonstick cooking spray.
9. Bake for 14 to 16 minutes or until all surfaces of the rolls are golden brown.
